geography.data_source: Census 2011 ward boundary shapefiles, South West Delhi district. Covers Dwarka, Janakpuri, Uttam Nagar, Najafgarh, Kapashera, Bijwasan. Largest Delhi zone by area — includes rural-urban fringe.

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 South West Delhi district tables. Density = 2300000 / 420 ≈ 5476. Low overall density due to large area including Najafgarh rural belt and Dwarka planned township with wide roads and open spaces.

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round MPCE distribution (South West Delhi). Broadly normal — Dwarka DDA flats are middle-income salaried class, Najafgarh side has more informal economy. Aviation economy from IGI Airport proximity (Kapashera, Bijwasan).

commute_profile.data_source: DMRC Origin-Destination Survey 2022, Census 2011 Table D-13. Dwarka is heavily metro-dependent due to distance from Central/South Delhi employment centres. Blue Line extension is the primary commute corridor. Long commute distance (16.2km) reflects suburban location.

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Composite scores. Moderate fare sensitivity (0.62) — middle-income but long commute makes fare a noticeable household expense. Lower political mobilisation (0.65) compared to dense inner-city zones — newer settlements with weaker community bonds.

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from IHDS-II social capital module. DDA flat structure creates moderate clustering. Newer settlements (Dwarka built post-2000) have weaker community ties than older colonies, reflected in lower connections (32) and clustering (0.52).

informal_economy_nodes.name: Janakpuri District Centre
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from DDA district centre data. Janakpuri West is a major commercial hub for Southwest Delhi with malls, offices, and retail.

informal_economy_nodes.name: Dwarka Sector 12 Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from MCD market data. Primary local market for Dwarka sub-city residential population.

informal_economy_nodes.name: Uttam Nagar Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from MCD market data. Dense commercial area serving the high-density Uttam Nagar residential belt — one of the most densely built-up areas in Delhi.

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: AGENT_SPEC.md Middle Income Residential template (lines 729-740, normalised to 1.00). Sum = 1.00. Formal sector employees dominate (0.31) — Dwarka is a bedroom community for Central Delhi office workers. Government employees (0.06) include Dwarka courts and NSIT/IGNOU campus staff.

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from MCD councillor count (13 wards), DDA RWA registration for Dwarka sectors, and school directory data. Higher RWA count (4) reflects active Dwarka RWA network.
